info.description: This problem set focuses on parasites and antiparasitic agents. The difficulty may vary a lot through out the set, since this set contains a mix of four different cognitive levels of complexity (recall, understand, apply, analyze). The questions are already reviewed, so mistakes should have already been minimized, but if you come across one, you can report through the Forms in the "Info and Links".

q1.prompt: A laboratory technician is identifying malarial species on a peripheral blood smear. Within erythrocytes of normal size, the technician observes large, comma-shaped reddish granules (3 to 8 per cell). These findings are the specific hallmark for which species?

q2.prompt: During a blood meal in an endemic region, an infected sandfly injects a specific developmental stage of *Leishmania* into the skin of a human host. Which of the following represents the infective stage for humans?

q3.prompt: A 24-year-old male presents with an intense, itchy rash on his wrists and finger webs that is worse at night. He reports having had a similar condition six months ago. His current symptoms appeared only 48 hours after his most recent exposure. Which of the following best explains the rapid onset of symptoms in this patient?

options.label: Direct toxic effects of mite protease
Delayed Type IV hypersensitivity upon reinfestation
Immediate Type I hypersensitivity to mite eggs

q4.prompt: A traveler returning from an endemic area is diagnosed with malaria caused by *Plasmodium vivax*. In addition to blood schizonticidal therapy, the physician prescribes primaquine. What is the primary clinical rationale for incorporating primaquine into this patient's management?

options.label: To eliminate active trophozoites within the red blood cells
To eradicate dormant hypnozoites in the liver to prevent relapse
To inhibit the further development of gametocytes in the bloodstream
To provide immediate protection against reinfection by the mosquito vector
To treat associated hemolytic anemia caused by the rupture of erythrocytes

q5.prompt: During a clinical microbiology lecture, a student asks about the acquisition of the "oriental lung fluke". The instructor explains that human infection occurs through the ingestion of a specific larval stage encysted within the tissues of a second intermediate host, such as a crab or crayfish. Which stage of this parasite life cycle is infectious to humans?

q6.prompt: A patient from an endemic region presents with spiking fevers, massive splenomegaly, and hyperpigmentation of the skin. Which of the following microscopic findings in a tissue sample would definitively identify the visceral form of this parasite?

options.label: Flagellated trypomastigotes in the peripheral blood
Fusiform promastigotes with an anterior flagellum
Trophozoite ring forms within red blood cells
Sarcocysts embedded within skeletal muscle tissue

q7.prompt: A 42-year-old patient presents with right upper quadrant pain and fever. An ultrasound reveals a large mass in the liver. Percutaneous drainage of the abscess obtains an exudate described as "anchovy paste-like" or "chocolate-like" pus. Which organism is most likely responsible for this finding?

q8.prompt: A laboratory technician is identifying malarial species on a thin blood film. The technician identifies infected erythrocytes containing characteristic crescent or "banana-shaped" gametocytes. Which of the following species is identified by this morphological hallmark?

q9.prompt: A patient is diagnosed with neurocysticercosis after presenting with new-onset seizures and cystic lesions in the brain. According to the life cycle of *Taenia solium*, which of the following exposures is the specific cause of this systemic form of the disease?

options.label: Ingestion of embryonated eggs in food contaminated with human feces
Ingestion of larvae encysted in undercooked pork
Ingestion of plerocercoid larvae found in raw freshwater fish
Ingestion of proglottids found in undercooked contaminated beef
Skin penetration by larvae present in contaminated soil

q10.prompt: Which of the following correctly pairs the infective stage and the diagnostic stage for *Cyclospora cayetanensis*?
